methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $104M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Simbe Robotics is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $104M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies including Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), and 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), plus one public comparable.

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $104M raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $177M. Peer-median analysis across the nine robotics comparables produces a $1.6B implied valuation, with the comp set ranging from $300M to $10.0B. The peer median reflects the broad valuation dispersion within the robotics sector at this stage.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $150M (conservative floor anchored to stage-progression) to $1.8B (upper bound), with a median of $888M. The substantial $1.65B spread reflects material uncertainty around whether Simbe's market position and near-term path merit valuation at the peer median or materially below it. Key sensitivities include the company's recurring revenue traction, gross margins relative to hardware-heavy peers, and timing to Series D. The stage-progression method alone suggests relative undervaluation versus peers; peer pricing dominates the triangulated midpoint.
